reason: INDEX MISMATCH IN REMARK (line 536): The inline expression describing the 3+1 conservation law writes partial_i[p^alpha (d gamma^BETA/dt) delta^{(3)}(...)] + partial_0[p^alpha (d gamma^0/dt) delta^{(3)}(...)]. The first term uses a Greek beta for the gamma index, but it should be Latin i to match the summation with partial_i. The divergence partial_beta T^{alpha beta} decomposes as partial_i T^{alpha i} + partial_0 T^{alpha 0}, so the spatial piece is partial_i[p^alpha (d gamma^i/dt) delta^{(3)}]. Using beta here creates a free Greek index that (a) does not contract with partial_i and (b) does not appear in the second term, making the expression ill-formed. Fix: change gamma^beta to gamma^i in the first term. This is not in the proof itself (only in a remark), so it does not affect the theorem's validity.
